cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. So the problem is about the International Olympic Committee (IOC) being concerned about something related to the 2008 Olympic medals. The task is to figure out what exactly they were worried about based on the provided text. First, I need to recall the relevant part of the passage. The question points out that the IOC had concerns when the jade ring design was proposed. Let me read through the passage again to find that section. Looking back, the passage says: "Experts from the International Olympic Committee (IOC) were concerned about the risk of fragility when the jade ring design was proposed. The IOC required that jade rings should be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test. The Shanghai Mint raised that standard to 3-meters. Medals can survive a 3-meter drop test with only minor cracks on the jade rings." From this, the key phrase is "risk of fragility." The IOC was worried that the jade rings might be too fragile. The next sentence confirms that they required the jade rings to be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test. The mint even increased it to 3 meters. So, the concern was about the jade being fragile and potentially breaking. Now looking at the options: (A) the medals will be worth less – The passage doesn't mention the monetary value or worth of the medals, so this is probably not the answer. (B) the medals are too difficult to make – The text does mention that the technology is not easy and involves strict quality controls, but the IOC's specific concern was about fragility, not the difficulty of manufacturing. (C) competitors don't like the design – There's no mention of competitors' opinions. The concern was technical, about
